cover.title: THE CANDLE
cover.subtitle: The one whose warmth stays steady, even when their feelings grow deeper.
cover.tagline: You're warm with everyone. The hard part is helping one person feel the difference.

profile_scores.caption: High Care and Expression, Low Pride. You're warm with everyone, while deeper feelings can be harder to show clearly.

short_summary: For one person, that warmth can slowly turn into something deeper.
You're feeling more than before. They just may not know what changed.

full_report.headline: THE CANDLE
full_report.intro: You're warm with everyone, including friends, coworkers, and people you just met. That warmth is real. The problem starts when your feelings grow deeper for one person, but the way you show them stays almost the same. This report is about how that difference gets lost, and how to make it clearer without becoming someone you're not.

chapters.title: When You Love
body: You're warm with everyone.
Friends, coworkers, even people you just met.
You take care of people. You speak gently. You make them feel welcome.
So when your feelings deepen for one person, the outside can still look exactly the same.
Inside, something has changed.
You think about them more. You care more. You want the relationship to mean more.
But from their side, it can still look like ordinary kindness.
You're clearly deeper in than before.
They just may not know what changed.
chapters.profile_note: High Care and Expression, Low Pride. You're warm with everyone, while deeper feelings can be harder to show clearly.

chapters.title: The Pattern You Keep Repeating: THE SAME TEMPERATURE CURVE
body: At the start of a relationship, your warmth draws people in fast.
You're kind. Easy to be around. Easy to trust.
Then your feelings grow, but your way of showing them barely changes.
You still check in. You still care. You still sound warm.
From their side, it can feel like nothing is different.
But inside, the difference is getting bigger.
You start wanting more closeness, more certainty, and a clearer place in their life.
Then when you finally show how much it matters, they may feel like it came out of nowhere.
chapters.highlight: You're not hurt because you cared too little. You're hurt because the depth changed before the signal did.

chart.title: THE SAME TEMPERATURE CURVE
chart.description: Your warmth stays steady on the outside while your feelings keep growing underneath.

chapters.title: What You've Gotten Wrong About Yourself

items.belief: I'm warm to everyone. That's just my personality.
items.truth: That's true. Warmth is part of who you are. But when your feelings change, the other person still needs something they can actually notice. Your personality can stay the same while your signal becomes clearer.
items.truth_short: Your warmth is real. Deeper feelings still need a clearer signal.

items.belief: If it's real, they'll figure it out eventually.
items.truth: They may sense something, but guessing isn't the same as knowing. Your usual level of warmth is already high, so they may not have much to compare it with. If the feeling matters, help them see what's different.
items.truth_short: Real feelings can still be missed when the signal looks familiar.

items.belief: If I treat them differently, they'll feel pressured.
items.truth: A small difference doesn't have to feel heavy. It can be as simple as saying, "I wanted to do this for you," or "You matter to me in a different way." Clarity is not the same as pressure.
items.truth_short: Showing a difference can be clear without becoming intense.

chapters.title: The Person You Keep Choosing
body: You're often drawn to people who enjoy your warmth but don't look very closely at where it comes from.
They like how comfortable you make them feel.
They may even notice that you care, but still assume you treat everyone the same way.
That can leave you doing more and more, hoping the difference will become obvious.
But effort doesn't always explain itself.
At some point, you need more than someone who receives your warmth.
You need someone who asks what it means, and you need to be willing to answer.
chapters.highlight: Warmth can open the door. Clarity is what tells someone they've been invited in.

chapters.title: What Actually Changes Something
body: What you need isn't to become warmer. You've already got that covered.
What you need is one clear difference.
Not a grand gesture. Not a secret test.
Just something that helps them understand this isn't how you feel about everyone.
You can say, "I wanted to do this just for you."
You can ask for time alone together.
You can tell them, "I've started thinking about you differently."
The point isn't to perform more. It's to make the meaning easier to see.
chapters.action_sentence: The point isn't to perform more. It's to make the meaning easier to see.

chapters.title: Where You Go From Here
body: You already know how to be warm. You know how to make people feel included.
What you're learning next is how to let one person feel the difference without pulling your warmth away from everyone else.
That doesn't mean becoming colder.
It means letting your words catch up with what your heart already knows.
You can stay kind to everyone and still be clear with the person who matters more.
chapters.transition_note: You don't need less warmth. You need a clearer way to show where your heart has gone deeper.

chapters.title: The Types You Keep Falling For

items.type_title: The Person Who Assumes You're Like This With Everyone
items.description: They enjoy your warmth, but they never stop to ask whether something has changed. You can end up trying harder instead of saying what the difference means.
They keep asking whether you treat everyone else the same way. That doubt can make you shrink your feelings instead of explaining them.

items.type_title: The Person Who Gets Used to Your Kindness
items.description: They can grow so familiar with your kindness that they stop noticing the choice behind it. Once that happens, the meaning can fade even while the care stays the same.

items.type_title: The Person Who Notices but Never Asks
items.description: They can tell something feels different, but they don't bring it into the open. Then both of you wait, and the relationship stays stuck in guesswork.

chapters.title: The Warning Signs You've Missed
chapters.intro_note: These signs can show up early. You may just keep brushing them off as part of your usual warmth.

signal_strength.label: They often ask, "you treat other people this well too, right?"
That hurts, but you brush it off with "that's just how I am"
Your feelings have clearly deepened, but your way of expressing them hasn't changed
They still receive your warmth as the "default"

chapters.core_signal: You already feel the difference. They still don't know what the difference means.

chapters.title: What a Healthy Relationship Looks Like for You

checklist.title: You don't have to rely on hints.
checklist.description: You can show the difference and say what it means without turning the moment into a big confession.

checklist.title: They ask instead of comparing.
checklist.description: They stay curious about your feelings instead of measuring themselves against everyone else.

checklist.title: Your kindness isn't taken for granted.
checklist.description: They notice the small things and understand that care still deserves care in return.

checklist.title: You feel special too.
checklist.description: The relationship doesn't leave you doing all the showing. Their feelings become clear to you as well.

chapters.title: What Happens In Your Next Relationship

sections.who_breaks_you: A hard match for you is someone who enjoys your warmth but never gets curious about what makes it different. They keep receiving, while you keep hoping the meaning will become obvious on its own.
sections.who_steadies_you: A steadier partner notices small changes and asks about them. They don't expect you to prove anything, and they don't leave you guessing about their feelings either.
sections.same_pattern_repeats: You may still feel drawn to people who compare, doubt, or take kindness for granted. That attraction isn't the part you need to fight. The real shift happens when both of you stop guessing and start saying what the relationship means.
sections.next_signs: The signs may show up when you want to do something a little different for them, then pull back because you're worried it will feel too obvious. Notice that moment. It may be the point where your feelings need words, not another hint.
sections.try_this: Choose one small difference and name it. Say, "I wanted to do this just for you," or "You've started to matter to me in a different way." Don't use it to test them. Just let the meaning be clear. Then notice whether the two of you can talk more openly about what is changing.
sections.final_line: What matters isn't one reaction. It's whether the difference between kindness and deeper feeling becomes easier to talk about.

action_plan.summary: You don't need to become warmer. You need one clearer way to show when your feelings have changed.
action_items: Do one small thing that feels personal, then say why you chose it for them.
Use one clear sentence instead of hoping the difference will explain itself.
Notice whether both of you become more open about what the relationship means.

closing.message: You've spent a long time being warm to everyone. This report looked at why deeper feelings could still be mistaken for ordinary kindness, and how one clear difference can help the relationship understand what changed.
closing.cross_sell_cta: Curious what your partner's pattern looks like? See their Cosmic Blueprint.
